chunks.text: Student Concerns and Grievances - EECS at Berkeley Student Concerns and Grievances The EECS Department is committed to building a broadly diverse community and strives to create a welcoming and respectful learning environment for students. Your feedb

tful learning environment for students. Your feedback and comments will help us work towards this goal. In this Page EECS Student Incident Reporting Form EECS General Student Grievances EECS Sexual Harassment Resources Other Resources for Discriminat

rassment Resources Other Resources for Discrimination Counseling and Mental Health Resources for Students Climate Feedback Form for Engineering EECS Student Incident Reporting Form Students are encouraged to use the EECS Student Incident Reporting Fo

aged to use the EECS Student Incident Reporting Form to submit incident reports or suggestions for improving the climate in the EECS Department. Submissions may be anonymous, or you may choose to provide contact information. This form is the result

ide contact information. This form is the result of a recommendation by the EECS Anti-Racism Committee and is intended to surface issues that may adversely affect the climate within the Department, such as those related to diversity, equity and hara

uch as those related to diversity, equity and harassment. Submissions will be reviewed by a team of EECS student services staff , including Audrey Sillers , Tiffany Grimsley and Antoine Davis ). You may also send mail to eecs-climate-report@eecs.berk

ay also send mail to eecs-climate-report@eecs.berkeley.edu EECS General Student Grievances Issues can arise between a faculty member and a student. For assistance please reach out to a staff advisor or the Student Ombuds Office. Additional campus res

r the Student Ombuds Office. Additional campus resources: Berkeley Student Advocate Office (SAO) : This campus office provides assistance on all manner of student grievances including, but not limited to, DSP accommodations, discrimination, sexual vi

to, DSP accommodations, discrimination, sexual violence, and non-traditional student concerns. EECS Sexual Harassment Resources Audrey Sillers , 203 Cory, 510-664-7181, araya@eecs.berkeley.edu Additional campus resources: PATH to Care : A confidenti

onal campus resources: PATH to Care : A confidential first point of contact for gender-based harassment or sexual violence. Office for the Prevention of Harassment and Discrimination : OPHD oversees campus compliance with policies prohibiting discrim

ampus compliance with policies prohibiting discrimination and harassment (including sexual harassment and violence). They are charged with responding to and resolving reports of harassment and discrimination from students, staff, faculty and visitors

ination from students, staff, faculty and visitors that are related to protected class and civil rights policies Other Resources for Discrimination Center for Student Conduct : The Center accepts incident reports on all manner of behaviors that viola

dent reports on all manner of behaviors that violate the campus Student Code of Conduct , including harassment. Office for the Prevention of Harassment and Discrimination : OPHD is charged with responding to and resolving reports of harassment and di

ding to and resolving reports of harassment and discrimination from students, staff, faculty and visitors that are related to protected class and civil rights policies. Berkeley Diversity : Report a specific incident or act of intolerance, hate, hara

pecific incident or act of intolerance, hate, harassment or exclusion at any of the Berkeley campus or UC-systemwide resources. Anti-Bullying/Abusive Workplace Policy and Resources Abusive Conduct Complaint Intake Form Counseling and Mental Health Re
